nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Radcliffe was born in 1273, in Bury, Lancashire, England, his father, Richard de Radcliffe, was 28 and his mother, Margaret le Boteler, was 24. He married Mary de Bury about 1294. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1315, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Radcliffe est né en 1273, à Bury, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Radcliffe, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margaret le Boteler, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Mary de Bury environ 1294. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1315, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Radcliffe 1273 in Bury, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Radcliffe 28 und seine Mutter Margaret le Boteler 24. Er hat um 1294 Mary de Bury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1315 im Alter von 42 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Radcliffe è nato il nel 1273 nel Bury, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Richard de Radcliffe, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Margaret le Boteler, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Mary de Bury circa 1294. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1315, all'età di 42 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Radcliffe(이)가 1273년 영국 랭카실르 버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Radcliffe(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret le Boteler(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1294년 Mary de Bury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1315년 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Radcliffe nasceu em 1273, em Bury, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Radcliffe, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margaret le Boteler, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary de Bury aproximadamente 1294. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1315, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Radcliffe родился в 1273 г. в местности бури, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Radcliffe было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret le Boteler, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary de Bury около 1294 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1315 г. в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Radcliffe nació en 1273, en Bury, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Radcliffe, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margaret le Boteler, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Mary de Bury aproximadamente 1294. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1315, a la edad de 42 años.
